conditional_states.condition: Chart renders progressively or has some series still loading.
Data is cached/polled and the chart may lag live values.
Large series take noticeable time to fetch and render.
User can select a series, point, or range to filter or drill down.
Chart should keep showing the last fetched data with a staleness marker offline.
Labels and legend must remain legible under zoom or large fonts.
Draw-in animation must degrade to an instant render.

rationale: Charts visualise data that may be absent, partial, or failed, so an explicit empty state (no data for this range) distinct from a load failure is required, alongside loading. Hover/focus tooltips expose exact values, and a keyboard/accessible path (focusable data points or an accompanying data table) is required because a canvas alone is invisible to assistive technology.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 1.1.1 (Non-text Content): charts need a text/table alternative for the data they encode.
WCAG 2.2 2.1.1 (Keyboard): interactive data points must be reachable without a mouse.
NN/g dataviz guidance: design explicit empty, loading, and error states for data visualisations.
